blocks.text: **Programming** is the process of giving a computer a precise set of instructions to follow in order to solve a problem or perform a task. A **program** is that set of instructions, written in a **programming language** that can be translated into machine code a CPU can execute.

blocks.title: Mental Model
blocks.text: Think of programming like writing a very precise recipe. The computer follows it exactly — no improvising, no common sense. Every step must be spelled out explicitly. If your recipe says 'add flour' but not how much, the recipe fails.

items: **Automation** — Turn repetitive manual tasks into one-click scripts
**Problem Solving** — Break down complex problems into smaller, solvable steps
**Career Opportunities** — Software development is one of the highest-demand careers worldwide
**Understanding Technology** — Everything runs on code: your bank, your phone, your car
**Creativity** — Build apps, games, websites, AI tools, automations

blocks.text: By tradition, the first program in any language prints 'Hello, World!' to the screen. Here it is in several languages to show how syntax differs but logic stays the same:

tabs.label: Java

blocks.title: Observation
blocks.text: Python needs 1 line. Java needs 5 lines. Both produce identical output. This illustrates the trade-off between **simplicity** (Python) and **explicitness/performance control** (Java, C).
Programming is really a structured approach to problem solving. Before writing a single line of code, experienced developers follow a mental process:

items.title: 1. Understand the Problem
items.description: What exactly needs to be solved? What are the inputs? What should the outputs look like? What are the edge cases?

items.title: 2. Plan the Solution (Algorithm)
items.description: Design a step-by-step procedure (algorithm) to solve the problem. You can write it in plain English (pseudocode) first.

items.title: 3. Write the Code
items.description: Translate your algorithm into a programming language. Focus on correctness first, then clarity, then performance.

items.title: 4. Test & Debug
items.description: Run the program with various inputs. Find bugs (incorrect behavior) and fix them. Test edge cases.

items.title: 5. Refine
items.description: Improve readability, performance, and maintainability. Good code is read far more often than it's written.

rows: Readable syntax, minimal boilerplate, huge community
Runs in every browser, immediate visual feedback
Strong typing teaches good habits; widely used
Teaches how computers really work (memory, pointers)
Simple syntax, fast compilation, great concurrency

blocks.title: The Language Trap
blocks.text: Don't spend months choosing a language. **The concepts you learn are transferable.** Loops, functions, data structures, algorithms — these exist in every language. Master the fundamentals in any language, then the rest comes easily.

points: Programming is giving a computer explicit, precise instructions to solve a problem
All languages have the same core concepts: variables, control flow, functions, data structures
The syntax differs between languages but the logic stays the same
Problem-solving process: understand → plan → code → test → refine
Pick any beginner-friendly language (Python or JavaScript) and start — switching later is easy
